description_long: This is a twin-engine propeller airplane with a high-wing configuration and a distinctive twin-boom tail design. The fuselage is primarily light gray, accented with bright red on the nose, wingtips, tail edges, and engine nacelles, as well as blue and white roundels and stripes on the tail fins. The engines are mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller, and the cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage with a clear canopy. The tail features two vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable or not modeled. The overall design is robust and functional, suitable for transport or utility roles.
description: A twin-engine, high-wing airplane with a twin-boom H-tail, gray fuselage, and red accents, featuring a forward cockpit and propeller engines.
description_view_0: Side view showing the high-mounted wings, twin engines, and the full length of the twin-boom tail with red accents.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the H-tail configuration, twin vertical stabilizers, and the broad wingspan.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other side of the fuselage and tail, with visible propellers and cockpit.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the nose, cockpit canopy, both engines with propellers, and the straight wing layout.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible dihedral or anhedral angle, no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston or turboprop engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; configuration indeterminate from images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-window canopy at the front of the fuselage.
